items.title: Carney tells Montreal Victoire players their PWHL championship is an 'inspiration'
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney praised Professional Women's Hockey League 2026 Walter Cup champions, the Montreal Victoire, for their dominating season. 'Follow your dreams, even if that dream is not possible yet,' said Victoire goaltender Ann-Renée Desbiens at the meeting with Carney.

items.title: Sabrina Carpenter granted temporary restraining order against man who showed up at her home
items.summary_auto: Pop star Sabrina Carpenter has been granted a temporary restraining order against a man who she says tried to force his way into her Los Angeles home “unannounced and uninvited,” in incidents she called "deeply alarming." In court documents obtained by CBC News, Carpenter’s lawyers say she is seeking a full restraining order against 31-year-old William Applegate.

items.title: Trump picks Bill Pulte to succeed Tulsi Gabbard, as acting director of national intelligence
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump has tapped Bill Pulte to be acting director of national intelligence, putting a real estate scion and fierce Trump loyalist in a key national security post as the U.S. remains at war with Iran.

items.title: Heavy rain brings challenges and benefits for Alberta farmers
items.summary_auto: Significant rainfall has left some farmers unable to continue seeding while others are welcoming the moisture.

items.title: 'Please say something': Sask. RCMP ask for public's help after fatal shooting in Pelican Narrows
items.summary_auto: The RCMP provided an update on the situation in Pelican Narrows, Sask., on Tuesday morning after an active shooter alert was active on Monday. One suspect is now in custody, after a woman died and a man was injured.

items.title: 'It turned into a circus': Milli Vanilli’s Fab Morvan latest artist to drop out of U.S. anniversary concerts
items.summary_auto: Milli Vanilli’s Fab Morvan is the latest artist to back out of the 250th-anniversary concerts in Washington, D.C., leaving just Vanilla Ice and Flo Rida out of nine acts that had been scheduled to perform.

items.title: Carney addresses technical recession, says economy going through 'settling-in' period
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney said his government is "fundamentally" transforming the economy as it responds to the U.S. trade war, and that the country's dip into a technical recession is partly a symptom of this "settling-in" period.

items.title: Mitch Marner Effect: How 1 decision put Golden Knights, Hurricanes on Stanley Cup final path
items.summary_auto: Holding the keys with a full no-movement clause, Mitch Marner nixed a trade to Carolina from Toronto late last season. His decision to join the Golden Knights as a free agent caused a domino effect that has led Vegas and Carolina to the Stanley Cup final.

items.title: Investigation into alleged RCMP misconduct against Indigenous girls in Prince George enters final stage
items.summary_auto: Alberta's police watchdog has been tasked with looking into historical accusations of wrongdoing.

items.title: Video showing Toronto police tackling cyclist after alleged traffic violation sparks outcry
items.summary_auto: Following an arrest downtown that was captured on video, Toronto police say a man now faces three charges after running a stop sign, swearing at officers and attempting to flee. But a Toronto lawyer specializing in cycling injury law says police may have used excessive force.

items.title: Quebec man who drowned his 2 children gets life in prison plus 9 years for assaults on wife, daughter
items.summary_auto: Kamaljit Arora was sentenced to life in prison without possibility of parole for 25 years, on Monday at the Laval, Que., courthouse, for the 2022 murders of his 13-year-old daughter and 11-year-old son.

items.title: Canada tells U.S., Mexico it wants CUSMA renewed
items.summary_auto: Canada has given the U.S. and Mexico official notice that it wants the free trade deal between the three countries to be renewed.

items.title: Iran reviewing proposed Trump deal to halt war as Lebanon attacks continue
items.summary_auto: Iran is reviewing a proposed agreement with the United States to halt the war between the two countries, Iran's Mehr news reported Tuesday after U.S. President Donald Trump said talks to reach a deal are continuing.

items.title: Trump issues proclamation to lower some steel, aluminum and copper tariffs
items.summary_auto: A proclamation signed late Monday by U.S. President Donald Trump will amend his ‌Sec. 232 national security tariffs on some aluminum, steel and copper imports, lowering them from 25 per cent to 15 per cent.

items.title: Here's what Hydro-Québec fought to hide from the Newfoundland and Labrador government
items.summary_auto: Hydro-Québec fought to hide parts of letters from the 1960s showing what it offered to lure a French aluminum company to the province, including its internal comments about an energy deal with Newfoundland and Labrador.

items.title: Researchers call for more collaboration to stop whale strikes in B.C.
items.summary_auto: In San Francisco Bay, scientists, ferry operators and the public are working together to track whales, using an app and a special kind of camera.

items.title: Denare Beach rebuilds, reflects on anniversary of devastating Sask. wildfire
items.summary_auto: Cautious optimism is mixed with concern in the northern Saskatchewan community of Denare Beach, where more than 200 homes burned in last year's Wolf Fire.

items.title: Women in farming more vulnerable to mental-health struggles and stress, University of Guelph study finds
items.summary_auto: A new study from the University of Guelph has found that women in farming are more vulnerable to mental-health struggles often tied to the visible and invisible workloads, and feelings of being undervalued. It's something that resonated with Jennifer Schooley, who runs a lavender farm in Norfolk County and speaks regularly about being a woman in agriculture.

items.title: Women in farming more vulnerable to mental-health struggles and stress, Guelph study finds
items.summary_auto: A new study from the University of Guelph has found that women in farming are more vulnerable to mental-health struggles often tied to the visible and invisible workloads, and feelings of being undervalued. It's something that resonated with Jennifer Schooley, who runs a lavender farm in Norfolk County and speaks regularly about being a woman in agriculture.

items.title: She was stabbed by a student while on the job. Now she’s fighting for support
items.summary_auto: The administrative assistant who was stabbed by a student at a Bedford, N.S., high school in 2023 is speaking out in an interview for the first time. Angela Light tells CBC News about how the horrific events of that day have impacted her life.

items.title: N.S. high school administrator, stabbed by student on the job, fights for support 3 years later
items.summary_auto: The administrative assistant who was stabbed by a student at a Bedford, N.S., high school in 2023 is speaking out in an interview for the first time. Angela Light tells CBC News about how the horrific events of that day have impacted her life.

items.title: Inquest into death of Ontario MPP's nephew hears from family, First Nation chief
items.summary_auto: Jonathon Mamakwa says he’s no longer angry about his son’s death, but that he wants to see more mental health and addictions support in correctional settings to prevent similar losses. Jonathon was the first witness to provide testimony in the coroner’s inquest into Kevin Mamakwa’s death, which began in Thunder Bay, Ont., Monday. Here's what's been shared so far about Kevin, a 27-year-old member of Kingfisher Lake First Nation who died June 2, 2020 while in custody at the Thunder Bay Jail.

items.title: Canada's cloud market is 'broken,' report warns
items.summary_auto: A new report calls the market for cloud computing in Canada "broken" and warns that without requiring compatibility between  shared processing providers, domestic alternatives to U.S. tech giants risk Canadians buying into "maplewashed dependencies."

items.title: How steep are World Cup ticket prices for locals?
items.summary_auto: The price of tickets has turned the World Cup into an elite spectacle and data shows just how costly the tickets are for the residents of the host cities.

items.title: 6 siblings forcibly disappeared in 2013 by Assad regime confirmed dead, family says
items.summary_auto: The family of Rania Al-Abbasi and Syrian authorities say that investigations have unveiled that her six children, who were detained along with Rania and her husband in 2013 by Syria's former Assad regime, are believed to be dead.

items.title: Mexican state uses AI law to arrest online critics
items.summary_auto: Authorities in the Mexican state of San Luis Potosí recently arrested two people linked to a Facebook page critical of the local government under a new artificial intelligence law that human rights and press freedom groups warn is being used to suppress political dissent.

items.title: After record-breaking Paralympics in Italy, IPC president sees room to grow
items.summary_auto: International Paralympic Committee president Andrew Parsons wants to see more women competing and more sports on the Paralympic program.

items.title: Manny Malhotra named new Vancouver Canucks head coach
items.summary_auto: The move is the latest in an overhaul that has seen Vancouver replace general manager Patrik Allvin, president of hockey operations Jim Rutherford, and head coach Adam Foote since finishing the season last in the league with a 25-49-8 record.

items.title: At least 18 dead, scores wounded as Russia launches attacks across Ukraine
items.summary_auto: Russia launched hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles against Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities overnight, killing at least 18 civilians and wounding more than 100 others, authorities said Tuesday.

items.title: What's at stake for upcoming CUSMA negotiations
items.summary_auto: The Canada-United States-Mexico Agreement is up for review on July 1. It's the free trade deal that impacts more than $1 trillion of the Canadian economy each year. Trump has previously threatened to abandon the agreement, but some trade experts are calling his bluff. Here's everything you need to know about the agreement, and what's at stake.

items.title: Laos cave rescuers race against time to find remaining missing men | Hanomansing Tonight
items.summary_auto: Divers in Laos are attempting to find two men trapped in a flooded cave network, days after one person was rescued and four others crawled to freedom. Cave diving expert Edd Sorenson says lack of access to clean water is one of many hazards they face.

items.title: Virus detected in invasive mosquitoes found in B.C.'s Sea-to-Sky region
items.summary_auto: Researchers found two mosquito species that tested positive for California serogroup virus, a group of mosquito-borne viruses that can cause encephalitis and meningitis in humans.

items.title: The Pentagon designates its press office 'classified space' — making it off limits to journalists
items.summary_auto: In another of a series of moves restricting media access at the Pentagon, the U.S. Defence Department has declared that its press office is now a classified space — and therefore inaccessible to journalists.

items.title: Trump administration to pause controversial anti-weaponization fund after court ruling
items.summary_auto: The U.S. Justice Department said Monday it will comply with a court ruling temporarily blocking a nearly $1.8-billion US fund meant to compensate allies of U.S. President Donald Trump, effectively agreeing to pause the plan for at least two weeks.
